title: You Opened a Credit Card. ICE Now Knows Where You Live
excerpt: Here's how your personal address travels from your credit card provider, through middlemen companies, and ends up with ICE.
content_html:
When you open a new credit card, you necessarily give the credit card company your address. You probably expect that information to remain with your bank.

But when you open a new credit card or change your address on file, your address leaves your credit card provider, travels through a web of middlemen companies, and ends up with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), which can then search, analyze, or pair that data with other pieces of information about your life without a warrant.

404 Media has mapped out this supply of data by reviewing U.S. government procurement records and internal documents from companies providing the information. It highlights the sometimes unexpected ways ICE is gaining access to peoples’ personal information as the agency continues to acquire and leverage all sorts of data. 

The agency is buying information not just for immigration enforcement but to also investigate what the administration describes as ‘voter fraud.’ ICE officials have also repeatedly attempted to intimidate legal observers of ICE activity.

“No one signing up for a credit card thinks they're giving data brokers a thumbs-up to sell their personal information to ICE. Not only is it an outrageous violation of our privacy, it's impossible for Americans to opt out,” Senator Ron Wyden told 404 Media in a statement.

sentences.zh: 当您开立新信用卡时，您必须向信用卡公司提供您的地址。

sentences.en: You probably expect that information to remain with your bank.
sentences.zh: 您可能希望该信息保留在您的银行中。

blocks.html_en: But when you open a new credit card or change your address on file, your address leaves your credit card provider, travels through a web of middlemen companies, and ends up with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), which can then search, analyze, or pair that data with other pieces of information about your life without a warrant.

sentences.zh: 但是，当您开立一张新信用卡或更改您备案的地址时，您的地址就会离开您的信用卡提供商，通过中间商公司网络，最终到达移民和海关执法局 (ICE)，后者可以在没有搜查令的情况下搜索、分析该数据或将其与有关您生活的其他信息配对。

sentences.en: ICE officials have also repeatedly attempted to intimidate legal observers of ICE activity.
sentences.zh: ICE 官员还多次试图恐吓 ICE 活动的法律观察员。

blocks.html_en: “No one signing up for a credit card thinks they're giving data brokers a thumbs-up to sell their personal information to ICE. Not only is it an outrageous violation of our privacy, it's impossible for Americans to opt out,” Senator Ron Wyden told 404 Media in a statement.

sentences.zh: “没有人在申请信用卡时认为他们是在向数据经纪人竖起大拇指，将他们的个人信息出售给 ICE。

sentences.en: Not only is it an outrageous violation of our privacy, it's impossible for Americans to opt out,” Senator Ron Wyden told 404 Media in a statement.
sentences.zh: 这不仅严重侵犯了我们的隐私，而且美国人也不可能选择退出，”参议员 Ron Wyden 在一份声明中告诉 404 Media。

content_html_zh:
当您开立新信用卡时，您必须向信用卡公司提供您的地址。 您可能希望该信息保留在您的银行中。

但是，当您开立一张新信用卡或更改您备案的地址时，您的地址就会离开您的信用卡提供商，通过中间商公司网络，最终到达移民和海关执法局 (ICE)，后者可以在没有搜查令的情况下搜索、分析该数据或将其与有关您生活的其他信息配对。

404 Media 通过审查美国政府采购记录和提供信息的公司的内部文件，绘制了这些数据的供应情况。 它强调了随着 ICE 不断获取和利用各种数据，该机构有时会以意想不到的方式获取人们的个人信息。

该机构购买信息不仅是为了移民执法，也是为了调查政府所说的“选民欺诈”。 ICE 官员还多次试图恐吓 ICE 活动的法律观察员。

“没有人在申请信用卡时认为他们是在向数据经纪人竖起大拇指，将他们的个人信息出售给 ICE。 这不仅严重侵犯了我们的隐私，而且美国人也不可能选择退出，”参议员 Ron Wyden 在一份声明中告诉 404 Media。
